title: Renewables and Low-Carbon Solutions
gist: This is a BCG energy-practice service/landing page rather than an analytical article—mostly a catalog of consulting offerings and proprietary tools across renewables, nuclear, hydrogen, CCUS, and biofuels. The only forward-looking analytical signals are surfaced via linked-article teasers (e.g., gas-plus-carbon-capture for data center power, nuclear scaling via execution).

insights.claim: BCG positions next-generation nuclear, hydrogen, carbon capture (CCUS), bioenergy, and geothermal as the frontier low-carbon technology set alongside mature wind and solar, with proprietary screening/benchmark tools to prioritize project portfolios.
insights.speaker: BCG Henderson Institute
insights.so_what: Maps the low-carbon investment opportunity set BCG is steering capital toward—useful taxonomy for energy-transition exposure.
insights.quote: frontier technologies such as hydrogen, carbon capture, bioenergy, and geothermal
insights.locator: Optimizing Go to Market and Monetization

insights.claim: BCG flags (via a linked article teaser) that gas plants with carbon capture are best placed to meet US data center power needs at scale through 2030.
insights.speaker: BCG Henderson Institute
insights.so_what: Links the AI/data-center power crunch to gas+CCS rather than pure renewables—relevant to power-demand and gas-infrastructure theses.
insights.quote: Gas plants with carbon capture are best placed to meet the needs of data center developers at scale through 2030.
insights.locator: Explore Our Insights — Solving the US Data Center Power Crunch
